title: OpenTelemetry JavaScript And Semantic Conventions Documentation
citation: OpenTelemetry JavaScript documentation and semantic convention documentation, accessed 2026-05-03.
permitted_uses: Performance-analysis and observability planning for TypeScript API, GraphQL, model, voice, MongoDB, and WebXR runtime events.
claims_supported: OpenTelemetry provides JavaScript APIs and SDKs for collecting telemetry data such as traces and metrics.
OpenTelemetry semantic conventions define common attribute and span naming guidance for HTTP and GraphQL.
OpenTelemetry JavaScript traces and metrics are documented as stable, while browser client instrumentation is described as experimental and less specified.
claims_not_supported: That OpenTelemetry instrumentation is zero-overhead or automatically safe for high-cardinality clinical payloads.
That a paid observability vendor is required for the local single-user pilot.
That browser/WebXR instrumentation semantics are as mature as server-side Node.js instrumentation.
